skill.name: env-and-assets-bootstrap
skill.description: Prepare conservative conda-first environment plans, asset path assumptions, cache hints, and setup notes for README-documented AI reproduction repositories.
skill.summary: Plan environments and assets before running an AI research reproduction repository.
skill.author: lllllllama

security_audit.summary: The high weak-crypto and backtick findings are false positives caused by ordinary prose, Markdown inline code, or descriptive text. The confirmed risk is that bundled scripts can execute conda, mamba, pip, venv, and shell wrapper commands against a target repository. No prompt injection, credential exfiltration, or malicious network behavior was found.

medium_findings.title: Package Manager Command Execution
medium_findings.description: The bootstrapper runs subprocess commands for conda, mamba, pip, and virtualenv setup. This is expected for an environment bootstrap skill, but it can execute untrusted setup logic from a target repository.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The subprocess call is direct and the command lists include package manager operations. The behavior is legitimate for the skill, but it carries normal supply-chain and setup-script execution risk.

low_findings.title: Caller Controlled Filesystem Writes
low_findings.description: The asset helper creates an assets directory and writes a manifest to the requested output path. This is a bounded utility behavior, but users should choose output paths carefully.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The code clearly creates directories and writes JSON files based on command-line options. There is no evidence of destructive writes or hidden file access.

low_findings.title: Static Pattern False Positives
low_findings.description: The weak-cryptography, reconnaissance, and Ruby backtick detections point to prose, Markdown inline code, command lookup, or ordinary descriptions rather than cryptographic code or shell injection.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review found no hashing, encryption, Ruby execution, or system reconnaissance at these locations. The matched text is documentation or Markdown formatting.

dangerous_patterns.title: subprocess.run Invokes Environment Setup Commands
dangerous_patterns.description: A helper function prints and then runs command arrays through subprocess.run with check enabled. The commands are assembled for environment creation and dependency installation.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The dangerous primitive is present and active when dry-run is not used. The command is argument-list based, which reduces shell injection risk.

content.value_statement: AI research repositories often need careful environment and asset preparation before any run. This skill turns README evidence into conservative setup notes, command plans, and asset assumptions.
actual_capabilities: Identifies common top-level environment files such as environment.yml, requirements.txt, pyproject.toml, and setup.py.
Builds conservative conda, mamba, or virtualenv setup plans from repository evidence.
Provides platform-specific virtualenv activation commands for Windows, macOS, and Linux.
Can run a bootstrap script with dry-run support for command review before execution.
Creates an asset manifest for common dataset, checkpoint, weights, cache, and output directories.
Extracts asset hints from README files and config directories when they mention datasets, checkpoints, or downloads.
limitations: It does not choose the reproduction target or interpret the research paper.
It does not verify that datasets or checkpoints are canonical or safe to download.
Running the bootstrapper may execute package setup logic from the target repository.
It does not manage containers, cluster schedulers, or custom CUDA builds by default.

use_cases.title: Prepare a New Reproduction Repo
use_cases.description: Create setup notes and asset assumptions before trying to run a newly cloned AI research repository.

use_cases.title: Review Environment Commands
use_cases.description: Generate a conservative command plan that separates documented steps from inferred fallback steps.

use_cases.title: Map Missing Assets
use_cases.description: List likely dataset, checkpoint, cache, and output paths so missing assets are visible before execution.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Setup Plan
prompt_templates.scenario: You have a target repository and want setup guidance.
prompt_templates.prompt: Prepare a conservative environment and asset setup plan for this README-documented reproduction repository. Include unresolved risks.

prompt_templates.title: Dry Run Bootstrap
prompt_templates.scenario: You want commands reviewed before they run.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the bootstrap guidance in dry-run mode first. Show the detected environment file, planned commands, and activation instructions.

prompt_templates.title: Asset Manifest Review
prompt_templates.scenario: You need to understand datasets and checkpoints.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create an asset preparation plan for the repository. Identify checkpoint, dataset, cache, and output directory assumptions from README and config evidence.

prompt_templates.title: Cross Platform Setup Assessment
prompt_templates.scenario: You need a setup plan for multiple operating systems.
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze the repository setup evidence and produce a conda-first plan with Windows, macOS, and Linux activation notes. Mark all inferred steps.

output_examples.input: A repository has environment.yml with a declared environment name.
output_examples.output: The skill reports the environment file, records the declared name, suggests conda environment creation, and provides activation instructions.

output_examples.input: A repository has requirements.txt but no conda file.
output_examples.output: The skill proposes a virtualenv fallback, adds platform-specific activation commands, and notes the fallback as adapted rather than documented.

output_examples.input: A README mentions checkpoint and dataset download paths.
output_examples.output: The skill records source hints, target asset paths, presence status, and unresolved questions about canonical sources.

best_practices: Review generated commands before execution, especially when the target repository is untrusted.
Prefer documented environment files before inferred dependency plans.
Record unresolved asset and version uncertainty instead of assuming missing details.
anti_patterns: Do not run bootstrap commands from an unreviewed repository with elevated privileges.
Do not treat inferred asset paths as canonical sources.
Do not use this skill as a full reproduction orchestrator or final results reporter.

faq.question: Does this skill run repository code?
faq.answer: It can run package manager setup commands when the bootstrapper is executed without dry-run. Review commands and target files first.

faq.question: Can it work without conda?
faq.answer: Yes. It can fall back to a virtualenv plan when conda or mamba is unavailable.

faq.question: Does it download datasets or checkpoints?
faq.answer: No. It prepares asset manifests and source hints, but it does not download or verify assets.

faq.question: Can it support Windows and macOS?
faq.answer: Yes. It emits platform-specific virtualenv activation guidance where practical.

faq.question: Is it suitable for generic Python setup questions?
faq.answer: No. It is scoped to README-first AI research repository reproduction work.

faq.question: What should I check before publishing output from it?
faq.answer: Check command provenance, dependency versions, asset sources, and any unresolved setup risks.
